summary: DHH recounts attending an amateur stand-up comedy night and breaks down the mechanical formula most comedians followed: unfunny setup, pause, punchline, laughs. He notes he's not criticizing the simplicity since it works, but observes that the most successful performer differentiated himself by adding a guitar. The brief post uses comedy as a lens to make a point about differentiation.
key_points: Most amateur stand-up follows a predictable four-step mechanical formula
Simplicity is not a flaw — it works in comedy as in other domains
The most successful performer differentiated himself by adding an unexpected element (guitar)
Being different doesn't guarantee success, but it improves the odds
money_quotes: Different isn't auto-bliss, but the odds are better.
key_insight: Following a proven formula works fine, but adding a differentiating element tilts the odds of standing out in your favor.
